medical_history.history_of_present_illness: Patient is a right handed woman with PMH significant for HTN and recent left subdural hematoma s/p left craniectomy. Presents with repeated episodes of transient aphasia and right hand weakness. Episodes start with right hand weakness followed by halting effortful speech, lasting for a few minutes. Occurred 3 times in the past 24 hours. Recent neurologic history includes syncopal incident, unsteady gait, right sided weakness, and word finding difficulty. MRI showed large L SDH collection. Craniectomy for evacuation performed, followed by increased Keppra dosage.
medical_history.past_medical_history: Hypertension, Depression, s/p L SDH evacuation
medical_history.family_history: Father with open heart surgery and subsequent post op stroke

physical_examination.general_examination: Vitals: T: 98 P: 70 R: 18 BP: 142/100 SaO2: 100% on RA. General appearance: alert, in no apparent distress. HEENT: Mucous membranes are moist. Left side scalp staples in place - wound is clean and dry.
physical_examination.specialty_examination: Neurological: Mental Status: Alert and oriented. Language is fluent with occasional word finding pause or stumbling. Cranial Nerves: II intact, V symmetrical perception, VII decreased activation of left forehead but symmetric facial movement. Motor: Significant right parietal drift. Strength R 5 in most muscle groups except significant drift in right upper extremity. Reflexes brisk. Sensory normal except for agraphesthesia and astereognosis prominent in the right hand.
